curation_notes: Re-curation with updated guidelines. Patch commit 18abeda fixes double-free in move_elements and move_all_elements implementations in src/traits.rs. The vulnerable code used ptr::read to read elements from arrays and boxed slices while calling a user-provided closure, then called forget(self) or Box::from_raw only after all elements were consumed. A panic in the closure would cause already-read elements to be dropped twice. Four implementations were affected: SliceMoveSource for arrays (line 867), SliceMoveSource for Box<T> (line 901), SliceMoveSourceCollection for arrays (line 1141), and SliceMoveSourceCollection for Box<[U]> (line 1178). The fix replaces unsafe ptr::read loops with a safe ArrayIter for array sources and into_vec() for boxed slice sources. Three non-test source files changed (traits.rs modified, array_iter.rs added, lib.rs module declaration), so noisy_patch is false. Vuln class use-after-free confirmed: CWE-415 (Double Free) is a specific form of use-after-free. No sibling files share this vulnerability pattern — all affected code is in src/traits.rs. Locations now include both move_elements and move_all_elements functions.
